course_description:
This course offers clinical experience in the assessment and management of either adult or paediatric clients. Students are given moderate-low levels of direction and support from on-site Practice Educators.

This course offers clinical experience in the assessment and management of either adult or paediatric clients. The focus of the clinical learning is on the integration of knowledge and skills in the description and analysis of communication disorders and the role of the student as a clinical practitioner. Students are given moderate-low levels of direction and support from on-site Practice Educators.

course_aims: This course complements the placement experience students gain in SPCH3113. Students join clinicians in their usual workplace, and will be expected to interview, assess, diagnose, treat clients, and report on assessment and intervention. Students are given moderate-low levels of direction and support from their on-site Practise Educators. Clinical skills and competencies will be assessed using Speech Pathology Australia’s Professional Standards for Speech Pathologists in Australia (2020) or the Competency Assessment in Speech Pathology (COMPASS®2) (Speech Pathology Australia, 2013).

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate culturally safe communication and interpersonal skills with clients, family members and other professionals and peers (SPA PS 1.1-1.7)
Apply clinical reasoning to select and administer appropriate assessments and analyse and interpret data to identify client communication and swallowing needs (SPA PS 3.2, 3.3)
Design and implement evidence-based, person- and family-centred management plans as required to support client communication and swallowing needs (SPA PS 1.1-1.7, 2.5, 3.4, 3.5)
Critically reflect on your professional abilities to set goals and professional development plans in the context of life-long learning (SPA PS 2.1, 2.2, 2.3)
Produce written and verbal clinical records which are appropriate to the clinical setting and the audience (SPA PS 1.2, 1.3, 1.5, 3.3)
Engage in interprofessional collaborative practice to achieve respectful partnerships across professions (SPA PS 1.4, 1.5, 1.7, 2.5, 2.6)

assessment_details.title: Clinical Placement
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be assessed by their practice educator at the end of their clinical placement using COMPASS® Online. Students are required to complete a self-evaluation prior to their meeting with their practice educator. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Practice Educators should complete their final evaluation of students using COMPASSﾮ Online one week following the completion of the placement.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Clinical Placement Students will be placed in a sessional speech pathology (internal or external) clinic with a practice educator. Students will be placed in a sessional adult or paediatric placement with a practice educator. Placement duration will be either: • 1 full day per week for 8 weeks of the semester or; • half a day per week for 13 weeks of the semester (Weeks 1-13). Start/finish times for placements will vary depending on the placement allocated to each student.
